questions.text: At the end of a laparoscopic procedure, which of the following is the most critical purpose of performing a "last look" throughout the abdomen?

answerOptions.text: A) To ensure all ports are removed
B) To locate obscure bleeding or visceral injury
C) To verify instrument counts
D) To allow spontaneous deflation of pneumoperitoneum

questions.explanation: A final, systematic inspection ensures that remote injuries (e.g., cautery burns, trocar trauma) or hidden bleeding are not missed.

questions.text: Which of the following techniques may reveal venous oozing during the final inspection?

answerOptions.text: A) Irrigating the abdomen
B) Applying Trendelenburg position
C) Slightly lowering pneumoperitoneum pressure
D) Flushing the CO₂ through the drain

questions.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum can tamponade venous bleeding; reducing pressure may reveal otherwise hidden venous ooze.

questions.text: Which is the most common source of unrecognized bleeding at the conclusion of laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Trocar injury to abdominal wall vessels
B) Bladder perforation
C) Ligation site hematoma
D) Bleeding from adhesions

questions.explanation: Vessel injury at trocar sites may be obscured by pneumoperitoneum and should be checked during desufflation and port removal.

questions.text: What is a recommended technique for placing a drain laparoscopically?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of a Veress needle
B) Pulling the drain through a port site with a grasper
C) Creating a new stab incision
D) Use of a robotic arm

questions.explanation: A common method is to insert the drain through an existing trocar site using laparoscopic assistance.

questions.text: What is a documented risk of placing a surgical drain through a laparoscopic port site?

answerOptions.text: A) Persistent pneumoperitoneum
B) Difficulty removing the drain postoperatively
C) Trocar site hernia due to inadequate fascial closure
D) Carbon dioxide reflux into the drain tubing

questions.explanation: Placing a drain through a 10 mm or larger trocar site can compromise fascial closure, increasing the risk of postoperative herniation or even evisceration. Proper closure of port sites is essential, especially when the drain is removed.

questions.text: Which of the following is a valid reason to close a trocar site fascia?

answerOptions.text: A) To reduce infection
B) To minimize postoperative ileus
C) To prevent hernia formation
D) To avoid CO₂ retention

questions.explanation: Fascial closure reduces risk of port site hernia, especially with large, non-dilating trocars.

questions.text: Which trocar size is typically considered the threshold for fascial closure?

answerOptions.text: A) 3 mm
B) 5 mm
C) 10 mm
D) 15 mm

questions.explanation: Trocars ≥10 mm often require fascial closure; 5 mm sites may be closed selectively in pediatric or high-risk patients.

questions.text: Which of the following port sites is most likely to require fascial closure?

answerOptions.text: A) 5 mm midline port in a healthy adult
B) 12 mm lateral port in an obese patient
C) 3 mm suprapubic port
D) 5 mm subcostal port with a dilating cannula

questions.explanation: Large ports, especially in obese patients, carry increased hernia risk and should be closed.

questions.text: Which of the following is least likely to be a risk factor for development of a trocar site hernia?

answerOptions.text: A) Pediatric age group
B) Use of non-dilating trocar
C) Obesity
D) Use of a blunt-tipped 5 mm dilating trocar in a healthy adult

questions.explanation: Fascial closure is typically not required for 5 mm ports, especially if a dilating trocar is used in a low-risk patient. Pediatric patients, obesity, and cutting trocars increase hernia risk.

questions.text: Which of the following techniques involves suturing the fascia from within the peritoneal cavity?

answerOptions.text: A) Open fascial closure
B) Laparoscopic-guided closure
C) Purely laparoscopic technique
D) Use of a trocar closure device

questions.explanation: This approach uses laparoscopic instruments to suture the fascia from inside the abdomen, but visualization may be limited.

questions.text: In the laparoscopic-assisted fascial closure technique, what is the purpose of passing the suture lateral to the trocar?

answerOptions.text: A) To minimize seroma formation
B) To avoid contact with muscle fibers
C) To ensure inclusion of fascia in the stitch
D) To allow for drain placement

questions.explanation: Proper lateral placement captures the fascia effectively for secure closure.

questions.text: Which of the following devices is commonly used for fascial closure via a skin-to-skin technique?

answerOptions.text: A) Monopolar hook
B) Keith needle
C) Veress needle
D) U-stitcher

questions.explanation: A Keith needle can be used to pass suture through both sides of a port site for closure.

questions.text: What is the recommended technique for fascial closure in obese patients when using an open method?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of blunt-tipped instruments only
B) Use of very curved needles and skin extension if needed
C) Avoid fascial closure entirely
D) Use of subcuticular stitch only

questions.explanation: Exposure can be challenging; curved needles and enlarging the skin incision can help reach the fascia.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes proper skin closure at the end of laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Always close with non-absorbable suture
B) Use only staples for rapid closure
C) Close with subcuticular stitch and/or Steri-Strips
D) Skin closure is not required

questions.explanation: Skin is often closed with subcuticular sutures or skin adhesives/tapes.

questions.text: Why is it important to evacuate CO₂ at the end of laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) To prevent incisional cellulitis
B) To enhance urinary output
C) To reduce postoperative shoulder pain
D) To prevent port site infection

questions.explanation: Retained gas irritates the diaphragm and phrenic nerve, contributing to shoulder pain.

questions.text: In which of the following situations should fascial closure be considered for a 5 mm port site?

answerOptions.text: A) Adult patient with BMI of 22
B) Use of a dilating trocar
C) Pediatric patient undergoing laparoscopy
D) Single 5 mm lateral port used for camera access

questions.explanation: Though fascial closure is generally unnecessary for 5 mm ports, it may be indicated in pediatric patients due to a proportionally thinner abdominal wall and higher relative hernia risk.

questions.text: Which feature of a non-dilating trocar increases the risk of port site hernia?

answerOptions.text: A) Blunt-tip entry
B) Use of a dilating inner obturator
C) Cutting mechanism that creates a fascial defect
D) Insertion at an angle

questions.explanation: Non-dilating, cutting trocars create a clean fascial defect that doesn’t self-seal, increasing the likelihood of hernia if not closed.

questions.text: Which of the following is a true statement regarding CO₂ gas retention post-laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) It is a marker of incomplete surgery
B) It is associated with increased risk of hernia
C) It is radiographically detectable for up to 3 days
D) It results in delayed gastric emptying

questions.explanation: Residual CO₂ may be visible on imaging following surgery but is typically clinically insignificant and self-resolving.

questions.text: What is the primary advantage of laparoscopic-guided fascial closure compared to purely laparoscopic closure?

answerOptions.text: A) Eliminates the need for suturing
B) Allows closure without skin incisions
C) Visual control while allowing external instrument use
D) Reduces hernia risk more than open closure

questions.explanation: The laparoscopic-assisted technique offers the advantage of visual control while allowing external instrument use to close the fascia securely.

questions.text: When using a Keith needle for fascial closure, which of the following is an important safety tip?

answerOptions.text: A) Use it only through 5 mm ports
B) Avoid reversing the needle intra-abdominally
C) Use laparoscopic grasper to direct the needle across the fascia
D) Use it to place tacking sutures for the drain

questions.explanation: A Keith needle is inserted from one side and manipulated intra-abdominally with a grasper to exit on the opposite side of the port site, allowing fascial closure without full skin extension.
